---
title: "Philanthropy Effectiveness Evaluation Tool"
description: "An interactive foundation-strategy dashboard — sector allocation, geographic focus, grant concentration, OECD DAC impact scoring, and an equity lens — built on a transparent, reproducible simulated dataset."
date: 2026-05-15
image: "../assets/project-philanthropy-thumb.png"
toc: true
---

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex;gap:0.5rem;flex-wrap:wrap;margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <span class="pill pill-blue">Foundation strategy</span>
  <span class="pill pill-sky">Impact evaluation</span>
  <span class="pill pill-teal">Data visualization</span>
  <span class="pill pill-slate">Stata · simulation</span>
</div>

<div class="proj-infobox">
  <div style="display:flex;gap:2rem;flex-wrap:wrap;">
    <div><div class="proj-label">Course origin</div><div class="proj-value">Philanthropy class, Georgetown McCourt</div></div>
    <div><div class="proj-label">Dataset</div><div class="proj-value">1,200 simulated grants</div></div>
    <div><div class="proj-label">Built in</div><div class="proj-value">One day, Stata + Claude AI</div></div>
    <div><div class="proj-label">GitHub</div><div class="proj-value"><a href="https://github.com/AnuritaS/philanthropy-tool" target="_blank">View code ↗</a></div></div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Where it started

Earlier this semester, I completed a comparative case study of Tides and The Kresge Foundation for my philanthropy class — analyzing how two of the most influential social- and environmental-justice funders in the U.S. deploy capital differently, and why it matters.

That research raised an obvious next question: what if this analysis could be interactive? What if a program officer, a researcher, or a curious student could explore the underlying data themselves, instead of reading it secondhand in a paper?

So I built it.

## What the dashboard does

A full foundation-strategy dashboard, designed and coded in a day, combining the grant research from the case study with data visualization:

- Analyzes sector allocation across NTEE codes (Candid's nonprofit classification system)
- Maps geographic focus and measures grant-size concentration
- Scores impact across the OECD DAC's six evaluation criteria
- Produces a grant strategy scorecard benchmarked against industry standards
- Tracks an equity lens — BIPOC-led organization prioritization — and its correlation with measurable outcomes
- Surfaces actionable recommendations for each foundation based on the above

## On the data

The dashboard runs on 1,200 simulated grants. The real underlying data lives in the academic case study itself; this build exists to show what a tool like this looks like in practice, not to republish the original research dataset. A companion Stata file documents exactly how the simulated dataset was constructed, so the methodology behind every chart is transparent and reproducible from scratch.

## Why this, why now

This sits exactly at the intersection I want to keep working in: policy research, program design, and the philanthropic sector — building tools that make evaluation legible to the people who actually have to act on it.

If you work in foundation strategy or impact evaluation, I'd welcome a conversation — including feedback on how to improve the dashboard.

<div style="margin-top:1.5rem;">
  <a href="https://github.com/AnuritaS/philanthropy-tool" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="card-link">View the repository →</a>
</div>
